steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The underlined claim states that the number of investigative articles has fallen overall since 2010. To justify this, the correct choice must compare a data point at or near 2010 to a later data point showing a lower number — one endpoint-to-endpoint comparison.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The newspaper published approximately 1,000 investigative articles in 2010 and approximately 500 in 2019.': Directly compares 2010 (start) to 2019 (end) and shows a drop from ~1,000 to ~500 — this is the endpoint comparison the claim requires. ✓
  • Choice B — 'The smallest annual number of investigative articles published in the newspaper during the period shown is approximately 1,600 in 2013.': Misreads the graph — 1,600 is the largest figure in the period, not the smallest, and a single mid-period year cannot establish an overall trend. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The greatest annual number of investigative articles published in the newspaper during the period shown is approximately 1,000 in 2017.': Misreads the graph — 1,000 is not the greatest figure, and one isolated year doesn't show an overall decline. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The newspaper published approximately 1,000 investigative articles in 2010 and approximately 1,600 in 2013.': Shows an increase from 2010 to 2013, which contradicts the claim of an overall fall. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
